title: Trace Completeness and Orphan Span Detection
app: Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector, Splunk Observability Cloud (APM)
spl:
index=traces sourcetype="otel:traces"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats dc(span_id) as span_count, dc(service_name) as service_count,
    sum(eval(if(parent_span_id="" OR isnull(parent_span_id), 1, 0))) as root_spans
    by _time, trace_id
| eval completeness=case(
    service_count==1 AND span_count==1, "single_span",
    root_spans==0, "orphan_no_root",
    root_spans>1, "multiple_roots",
    1==1, "complete")
| stats count as trace_count,
    sum(eval(if(completeness=="single_span",1,0))) as single_spans,
    sum(eval(if(completeness=="orphan_no_root",1,0))) as orphans,
    sum(eval(if(completeness=="multiple_roots",1,0))) as multi_root
    by _time
| eval completeness_pct=round((trace_count - single_spans - orphans - multi_root)*100/trace_count, 1)
| table _time, trace_count, completeness_pct, single_spans, orphans, multi_root
description: Incomplete traces вАФ missing parent spans, single-span traces from multi-service flows, or orphaned spans with no root вАФ indicate broken context propagation, missing instrumentation, or sampling inconsistencies. These gaps make distributed debugging impossible exactly when it matters most. Measuring trace completeness quantifies instrumentation quality and identifies which services need propagation fixes.
value: Incomplete traces вАФ missing parent spans, single-span traces from multi-service flows, or orphaned spans with no root вАФ indicate broken context propagation, missing instrumentation, or sampling inconsistencies. These gaps make distributed debugging impossible exactly when it matters most. Measuring trace completeness quantifies instrumentation quality and identifies which services need propagation fixes.
implementation: Analyze trace structure by examining parent-child span relationships within each trace_id. Classify traces: "complete" (single root, proper parent chain), "single_span" (only one span вАФ missing downstream instrumentation), "orphan_no_root" (no span has an empty parent_span_id вАФ broken propagation), "multiple_roots" (more than one root span вАФ context fragmentation). Track completeness percentage over time. Alert when completeness drops below 90%. Identify which services produce the most orphan spans by joining back to service_name. For tail-sampling deployments, validate that sampling decisions are consistent across services (UC-13.3.7 covers sampling rate; this UC covers the resulting trace quality).
visualization: Line chart (completeness % over 7 days), Pie chart (trace classification breakdown), Table (services producing most orphan spans), Single value (current completeness %).

references.title: Splunk Lantern вАФ use case library

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
вАҐ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector, Splunk Observability Cloud (APM).
вАҐ Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=otel:traces`, `index=traces`.
вАҐ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 вАФ Configure data collection
Analyze trace structure by examining parent-child span relationships within each trace_id. Classify traces: "complete" (single root, proper parent chain), "single_span" (only one span вАФ missing downstream instrumentation), "orphan_no_root" (no span has an empty parent_span_id вАФ broken propagation), "multiple_roots" (more than one root span вАФ context fragmentation). Track completeness percentage over time. Alert when completeness drops below 90%. Identify which services produce the most orphan spвА¶

Step 2 вАФ Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=traces sourcetype="otel:traces"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats dc(span_id) as span_count, dc(service_name) as service_count,
    sum(eval(if(parent_span_id="" OR isnull(parent_span_id), 1, 0))) as root_spans
    by _time, trace_id
| eval completeness=case(
    service_count==1 AND span_count==1, "single_span",
    root_spans==0, "orphan_no_root",
    root_spans>1, "multiple_roots",
    1==1, "complete")
| stats count as trace_count,
    sum(eval(if(completeness=="single_span",1,0))) as single_spans,
    sum(eval(if(completeness=="orphan_no_root",1,0))) as orphans,
    sum(eval(if(completeness=="multiple_roots",1,0))) as multi_root
    by _time
| eval completeness_pct=round((trace_count - single_spans - orphans - multi_root)*100/trace_count, 1)
| table _time, trace_count, completeness_pct, single_spans, orphans, multi_root
```

Understanding this SPL

**Trace Completeness and Orphan Span Detection** вАФ Incomplete traces вАФ missing parent spans, single-span traces from multi-service flows, or orphaned spans with no root вАФ indicate broken context propagation, missing instrumentation, or sampling inconsistencies. These gaps make distributed debugging impossible exactly when it matters most. Measuring trace completeness quantifies instrumentation quality and identifies which services need propagation fixes.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=otel:traces`, `index=traces`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector, Splunk Observability Cloud (APM). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedвАФrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: traces; **sourcetype**: otel:traces. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

вАҐ Scopes the data: index=traces, sourcetype="otel:traces". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
вАҐ Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
вАҐ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time, trace_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
вАҐ `eval` defines or adjusts **completeness** вАФ often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
вАҐ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
вАҐ `eval` defines or adjusts **completeness_pct** вАФ often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
вАҐ Pipeline stage (see **Trace Completeness and Orphan Span Detection**): table _time, trace_count, completeness_pct, single_spans, orphans, multi_root


Step 3 вАФ Validate
Replay with a small load change. Compare with the OTel collector logs, pipeline dashboards, and health panels for the same labels.

Step 4 вАФ Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (completeness % over 7 days), Pie chart (trace classification breakdown), Table (services producing most orphan spans), Single value (current completeness %).
grandmaExplanation: We watch application traces, real users, and service-level health so we see slow, wrong, and costly behavior in the same story our teams ship to.
knownFalsePositives: Latency spikes during pool exhaustion, garbage collection, or upstream slowness. We compare with the APM UI and deploy history before a code-only theory.
